name: Overlay
description: A floating surface for contextual content that appears above the page, such as popovers and floating panels. Powered by floating-ui for intelligent placement, automatic repositioning, and focus management.
import: import { Overlay, OverlayTrigger, OverlayPanel, OverlayPanelContent, OverlayHeader, OverlayPanelCloseButton } from "@salt-ds/core";

Overlay.description: Root component managing overlay state, positioning via floating-ui, focus trap, and interactions. Provides context to child components.

open.description: Controlled: whether overlay is visible. When undefined, overlay is uncontrolled with initial closed state.

onOpenChange.description: Callback when overlay open state changes (trigger activation, ESC key, click outside, or close button).

placement.description: Preferred placement of overlay relative to trigger. Automatically repositions via flip() middleware if overflow detected.

children.description: Must contain OverlayTrigger and OverlayPanel as children.

internals.interactions: Uses floating-ui useClick (trigger activation), useDismiss (ESC, click outside), useRole (dialog role), focus management

OverlayTrigger.description: Wraps the trigger element (typically Button). Clones single React child with floating ref and interaction handlers attached.

children.description: The element that opens the overlay on activation. Must be a Button or similar focusable interactive element.

OverlayPanel.description: Floating panel container positioned via floating-ui. Renders FloatingArrow. Should contain OverlayHeader/OverlayPanelContent and optional close button.

children.description: Panel content: typically OverlayHeader or OverlayPanelContent, and OverlayPanelCloseButton or actions in OverlayHeader.

OverlayPanelContent.description: Wrapper for the main content area within OverlayPanel.

OverlayHeader.description: Structured header component for overlay panel (recommended over manual header). Includes title, description, and action buttons.

actions.description: Action buttons (close button, etc.) rendered in header. Preferred over deprecated OverlayPanelCloseButton.

OverlayPanelCloseButton.description: DEPRECATED. Close button for overlay. Use OverlayHeader with actions prop instead.

states.open: Overlay visible, focus entered into panel, overlay content accessible, ESC/click-outside/close-button closes.
states.closed: Overlay hidden, trigger receives focus, overlay not in DOM (portal removed).

focusManagement.onOpen: Focus automatically enters overlay panel (OverlayHeader close button receives initial focus if present).
focusManagement.onClose: Focus returns to trigger element. Overlay removed from viewport/DOM.
focusManagement.focusTrap: Tab/Shift+Tab cycles within overlay; focus does not escape to page during open state.

keyboard.Escape: Closes overlay from anywhere in content, returns focus to trigger.
keyboard.Enter/Space (close button focused): Closes overlay (OverlayHeader actions or deprecated OverlayPanelCloseButton).
keyboard.Tab (close button focused): Moves focus to first focusable content element. From last content element, wraps to close button.
keyboard.Shift+Tab (close button focused): Moves focus to last focusable content element. From first content element, wraps to close button.
keyboard.Click outside panel: Closes overlay (dismiss behavior via floating-ui useDismiss).

aria: aria-expanded: set on trigger (managed by Overlay context, indicates open/closed state)
aria-haspopup: inferred by floating-ui for dialog role
id: use id on Overlay for form association with screen readers
id-header: on OverlayHeader or content header for screen reader heading semantics
id-content: on content description for screen reader context linking

composition.typical_structure: Overlay > { OverlayTrigger (Button), OverlayPanel > { OverlayHeader (or OverlayPanelContent), optional close actions } }
with: Checkbox, Radio, Input (form controls in content)
composition.nesting: Do not nest Overlay inside Overlay. Use Dialog for modal confirmation instead. Use Drawer for persistent side panels.

patterns.filter_panel: Overlay shows filterable options, user selects/applies, overlay remains open until explicit close or action.
patterns.dropdown_panel: Overlay shows options, selection auto-closes overlay.
patterns.popover_info: Overlay shows supplemental info or related actions. User-initiated close (button or ESC).
patterns.contextual_menu: Overlay positioned near trigger, shows related actions. Close via selection or dismiss.
